+{"id":"gt-5uf3","title":"Patrol step: check parked molecules for unblock","description":"Add patrol step to Deacon for checking parked molecules:

```yaml
- step: check-parked-molecules
  action: |
    For each molecule with:
      - status: in_progress
      - assignee: null
      - has step with external: blocked_by
    Check if external deps are now satisfied.
    If yes: spawn polecat to resume the molecule.
```

This automates the resume process - no manual intervention needed when
upstream dependencies ship.

Part of cross-project dependency system.
See: docs/cross-project-deps.md

Depends on: gt-in3x (spawn --continue)

Add Aliases: []string{"wit"} to witnessCmd.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T23:11:53.453692-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T23:11:53.453692-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-5wb7","title":"Update vision.md with proto/mol/wisp terminology","description":"Update the Steam Engine Metaphor section to use consistent phase terminology:

Current (vision.md):
- Proto molecules = fuel (templates)
- Wisps = steam (transient execution traces)  
- Digests = distillate (permanent records)

New terminology:
- **Proto** = crystal/solid - frozen template
- **Mol** = liquid - reified durable instance (tracked in git)
- **Wisp** = gas - ephemeral instance (evaporates after squash)
- **Digest** = distillate - compressed summary after squash

Key clarification needed:
- Proto → bond → creates either Mol (durable) or Wisp (ephemeral)
- The choice of Mol vs Wisp depends on --wisp flag
- Default is Mol (durable, recorded in main beads)
- Wisp lives in .beads-ephemeral/, squashes away","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:32:45.68234-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:20:42.826322-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wb7","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.307488-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-5wtw","title":"Shutdown request handler","description":"Handle polecat shutdown requests:

When polecat runs 'gt handoff --shutdown':
1. Polecat sends mail to <rig>/witness requesting shutdown
2. Witness receives mail
3. Witness verifies:
   - Git working tree is clean
   - Work is submitted (MR exists or in queue)
   - No uncommitted beads changes
4. If clean:
   - gt session stop <rig>/<polecat>
   - git worktree remove polecats/<name>
   - git branch -d polecat/<name>
5. If not clean:
   - Send nudge back to polecat
   - Track retry count

Depends on fixing gt-dsfi (handoff deadlock).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:22.968711-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:28:15.512143-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-53w6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:37.300578-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-5wtw","depends_on_id":"gt-mxyj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:14:38.893061-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-h5n.7","title":"Auto-target: MRs for epic children target integration branch","description":"Automatically target integration branches for epic children.

When 'gt mq submit' is called:
1. Parse source issue from branch
2. Check if issue has a parent epic
3. Check if integration/<epic> branch exists
4. If yes: set target=integration/<epic>
5. If no: set target=main

This ensures batch work automatically flows to integration branches.

Also update 'gt mq submit --epic' to explicitly target an epic's integration branch.

Reference: docs/merge-queue-design.md#integration-branches","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:51:56.992465-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T11:59:13.982658-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T11:59:13.982658-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-h5n.7","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:51:56.994579-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-h5n.7","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:53:16.648727-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-h5n.8","title":"MQ config schema: merge_queue section in rig config.json","description":"Define and implement merge_queue configuration in rig config.

Schema:
{
  "merge_queue": {
    "enabled": true,
    "target_branch": "main",
    "integration_branches": true,
    "on_conflict": "assign_back",  // or "auto_rebase"
    "run_tests": true,
    "test_command": "go test ./...",
    "delete_merged_branches": true,
    "retry_flaky_tests": 1,
    "poll_interval": "30s",
    "max_concurrent": 1
  }
}

Implement:
- Config loading in rig package
- Default values
- Validation

Reference: docs/merge-queue-design.md#configuration","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:52:00.322779-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.864359-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.864359-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-h5n.8","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:52:00.324781-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-h5n.9","title":"Per-epic config overrides: custom merge settings","description":"Allow per-epic merge configuration overrides.

Epic can specify merge_config in its description:
  merge_config:
    run_tests: true
    test_command: 'go test -race ./...'
    on_conflict: assign_back

When processing MRs for an epic:
1. Load rig-level merge_queue config
2. Check if epic has merge_config
3. Merge epic config over rig config
4. Use merged config for processing

Use cases:
- Risky refactors: more thorough testing
- Urgent fixes: skip some checks
- Experimental work: different test suite

Reference: docs/merge-queue-design.md#per-epic-overrides","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:52:02.32832-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T13:52:02.32832-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-h5n.9","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:52:02.32997-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-hcc0","title":"gt polecat remove --all: bulk polecat teardown","description":"Currently gt polecat remove only accepts one polecat at a time. Need bulk operations:

## Requested
- `gt polecat remove gastown --all` - remove all polecats from a rig
- `gt polecat remove gastown/A gastown/B ...` - remove multiple by name

## Context
After a swarm completes, tearing down 20 polecats one at a time is tedious.
Ephemeral workers should be easy to create and destroy in bulk.

 {"id":"gt-iib","title":"Architecture: Decentralized rig structure with per-rig agents","description":"## Decision

Adopt decentralized architecture where each rig contains all its agents (mayor/, witness/, refinery/, polecats/) rather than centralizing mayor clones at town level.

## Town Level Structure

```
~/ai/                              # Town root
├── config/                        # Town config (VISIBLE, not hidden)
│   ├── town.json                  # {"type": "town"}
│   ├── rigs.json                  # Registry of managed rigs
│   └── federation.json            # Wasteland config (future)
│
├── mayor/                         # Mayor's HOME at town level
│   ├── CLAUDE.md
│   ├── mail/inbox.jsonl
│   └── state.json
│
└── <rigs>/                        # Managed projects
```

## Rig Level Structure (e.g., wyvern)

```
wyvern/                            # Rig = clone of project repo
├── .git/info/exclude              # Gas Town adds: polecats/ refinery/ witness/ mayor/
├── .beads/                        # Beads (if project uses it)
├── [project files]                # Clean project code on main
│
├── polecats/                      # Worker clones
│   └── <name>/                    # Each is a git clone
│
├── refinery/
│   ├── rig/                       # Refinery's clone
│   ├── state.json
│   └── mail/inbox.jsonl
│
├── witness/                       # NEW: Per-rig pit boss
│   ├── rig/                       # Witness's clone
│   ├── state.json
│   └── mail/inbox.jsonl
│
└── mayor/
    ├── rig/                       # Mayor's clone for this rig
    └── state.json
```

## Key Decisions

1. **Visible config dir**: `config/` not `.gastown/` (models don't find hidden dirs)
2. **Witness per-rig**: Each rig has its own Witness (pit boss) with its own clone
3. **Mayor decentralized**: Mayor's clones live IN each rig at `<rig>/mayor/rig/`
4. **Minimal invasiveness**: Only `.git/info/exclude` modified, no commits to project
5. **Clone subdir name**: Keep `rig/` for consistency (refinery/rig/, witness/rig/, mayor/rig/)

## Role Detection

- Town root or mayor/ → Mayor (town level)
- Rig root → Mayor (canonical main)
- <rig>/mayor/rig/ → Mayor (rig-specific)
- <rig>/refinery/rig/ → Refinery
- <rig>/witness/rig/ → Witness
- <rig>/polecats/<name>/ → Polecat

## Migration from PGT

- `mayor/rigs/<rig>/` → `<rig>/mayor/rig/`
- `<rig>/town/` → eliminated (rig root IS the clone)
- Add `witness/` to each rig","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:19.913928-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.461186-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.461186-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-iib","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.374551-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-in3x","title":"gt spawn --continue for resuming parked molecules","description":"Add `--continue` flag to gt spawn for resuming parked molecules:

```bash
gt spawn --continue gt-mol-root
```

This:
1. Finds molecule root
2. Verifies molecule is in "parked" state (in_progress, no assignee)
3. Checks external deps are now satisfied
4. Spawns polecat with molecule context
5. Polecat reads handoff mail and continues from blocked step

Part of cross-project dependency system.
See: docs/cross-project-deps.md

 {"id":"gt-iu23","title":"Polecat doesn't auto-start after spawn inject - requires manual nudge","description":"## Problem

After `gt spawn --issue <id> --create`, the polecat session shows Claude Code started with the injected prompt, but Claude doesn't begin processing. The prompt just sits there until manually nudged.

## Evidence

```
$ gt spawn --issue gt-rixa --rig gastown --create
...
✓ Session started. Attach with: gt session at gastown/furiosa
  Polecat nudged to start working
  Witness notified to monitor startup
```

Session shows:
```
> You have a work assignment. Run 'gt mail inbox' to see it, then start
  working on issue gt-rixa.

  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)
```

But Claude doesn't respond. Manual nudge required:
```
$ gt nudge gt-gastown-furiosa "Please start working..."
```

After nudge, polecat immediately starts working correctly.

## Hypothesis

The spawn inject happens before Claude Code is fully initialized. The text arrives in the input buffer, but Claude hasn't started listening yet. By the time Claude starts, the input has already been 'consumed' as initial prompt text but not submitted.

## Resolution Plan

This will be solved by the **polecat molecule workflow** (mol-polecat-work), which provides structured lifecycle management. The molecule approach handles startup, work, and shutdown as discrete steps with proper state tracking.

**Blocked on**: beads/crew/dave completing ephemeral molecules (bd mol bond, ephemeral beads repo).

## Workaround

For now, use `gt nudge` if a polecat doesn't start within ~30 seconds of spawn.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:06:27.375686-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:53:46.940942-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-zly","title":"Swarm learning: Beads database locality gap","description":"## PGT Bug (GGT Design Already Correct)

Mayor created issues in `~/ai/mayor/rigs/beads/.beads/` but polecats use `~/ai/beads/.beads/`. Different databases = polecats can't see Mayor's beads.

**GGT Fix**: architecture.md already specifies:
- All agents use BEADS_DIR pointing to rig-level `.beads/`
- Lines 116-143: Beads Configuration for Multi-Agent
- Lines 573-586: Rig-Level Beads via BEADS_DIR

This is a PGT implementation gap, not a design issue. GGT spawn must set BEADS_DIR correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:21:45.37072-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T01:28:40.74469-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T01:28:40.74469-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-zniu","title":"gt park command for parking molecules on external deps","description":"Add `gt park` command for when polecat hits external dependency:

```bash
gt park --step=gt-mol.3 --waiting="beads:mol-run-assignee"
```

This command:
1. Adds blocked_by: external:beads:mol-run-assignee to the step
2. Clears assignee on the step
3. Clears assignee on molecule root
4. Sends handoff mail to self with context
5. Shuts down polecat session

The molecule enters "parked" state (derived: in_progress + no assignee + blocked step).

Part of cross-project dependency system.
See: docs/cross-project-deps.md

Depends on Beads: bd-om4a (external: blocked_by support)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:39:01.401567-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T22:39:01.401567-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-zr0a","title":"bd pin command fails: invalid field for update: pinned","description":"In beads v0.32.0, `bd pin <id>` fails with 'invalid field for update: pinned'. The pinned field exists in the schema but update logic doesn't handle it.

```
bd pin <id>
# Error: invalid field for update: pinned
```

Expected: Issue should be pinned.

This blocks gt mail send --pinned from working.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T19:35:29.927326-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:20:33.909055-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:20:33.909055-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed by adding pinned to allowedUpdateFields"}
 {"id":"gt-zut3","title":"Immediate daemon notification on lifecycle request","description":"When gt handoff sends a lifecycle request to the daemon (via bd mail send deacon/), the daemon only discovers it on its next heartbeat poll (every 5 min). Workers wait unnecessarily for retirement.

## Current Behavior
1. Worker runs gt handoff
2. Lifecycle mail sent to deacon/ via beads
3. Worker blocks waiting for retirement
4. Daemon heartbeat runs (up to 5 min later)
5. Daemon processes lifecycle request and kills session

## Expected Behavior
Lifecycle requests should be processed immediately, not on poll interval.

## Proposed Solutions

### Option A: SIGUSR1 Signal (simplest)
1. Add SIGUSR1 handler in daemon that calls ProcessLifecycleRequests()
2. gt handoff reads daemon.pid and sends SIGUSR1 after mail send

### Option B: Unix Socket
1. Daemon listens on ~/gt/daemon/lifecycle.sock
2. gt handoff connects and sends 'process' message

### Option C: Watchfile + fsnotify
1. gt handoff touches ~/gt/daemon/lifecycle.trigger
2. Daemon uses fsnotify to detect and process

Recommend Option A for simplicity.

## Affected Files
- internal/daemon/daemon.go - add signal handler
- internal/cmd/handoff.go - send signal after mail","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:20:07.616335-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:27:42.419089-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:27:42.419089-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-zx3","title":"Per-rig beads repo configuration","description":"Add per-rig beads configuration to rig config schema.

## Config Schema

In each rig's config.json:

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "name": "wyvern",
  "git_url": "https://github.com/steveyegge/wyvern",
  "beads": {
    "repo": "local",    // "local" | "<path>" | "<git-url>"
    "root": null,       // Override bd --root (optional)
    "prefix": "wyv"     // Issue prefix for this rig
  }
}
```

## Repo Options

| Value | Meaning | Use Case |
|-------|---------|----------|
| `"local"` | Use project's `.beads/` | Own projects, full commit access |
| `"<path>"` | Use beads at path | OSS contributions |
| `"<git-url>"` | Clone and use repo | Team shared beads |

## Environment Injection

When spawning polecats, Gas Town sets:
```bash
export BEADS_ROOT="<resolved-path>"
```

## Resolution Logic

```go
func ResolveBeadsRoot(rigConfig *RigConfig, rigPath string) (string, error) {
    beads := rigConfig.Beads
    switch {
    case beads.Root != "":
        return beads.Root, nil
    case beads.Repo == "local" || beads.Repo == "":
        return filepath.Join(rigPath, ".beads"), nil
    case strings.HasPrefix(beads.Repo, "/"):
        return beads.Repo, nil
    case strings.Contains(beads.Repo, "://"):
        return cloneAndResolve(beads.Repo)
    default:
        return filepath.Join(rigPath, beads.Repo), nil
    }
}
```

## Backwards Compatibility

If `beads` section missing, assume `"repo": "local"`.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:16.660049-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:02.122203-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-zx3","depends_on_id":"gt-l3c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:47:35.726502-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
